codeReview.summary: Commit b671b63 correctly removes the redundant size validation block (8 lines) from WriteFileTool::execute() that ran AFTER the guardrail validate_file_size() with a different default (10 MiB vs 1 MiB). The single file size validation now lives in the guardrail function only, using consistent 1 MiB default with proper config-driven behavior.

summary: Fix review PASS. The commit b671b63 correctly removes 8 lines of dead code from tools.rs that represented the redundant second size check. After the fix, WriteFileTool::execute() only calls validate_file_size() from file_guardrails.rs once, using the consistent default of 1 MiB. The guardrail properly respects enforce_size_limit=false to skip the check. The self.max_size field remains on the struct for ReadFileTool (used for file read size limits), but WriteFileTool no longer uses it after the guardrail refactor. All 31 file_guardrail tests pass.
